One Sentence Summary

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Certain primate models demonstrated that the brain RNA correlations with human alcohol use disorder (AUD) were approximately 40% as strong as the correlations observed within human samples themselves

findings.claim: Single-cell transcriptomic analyses of human and mouse cortex show an increased diversity of glutamatergic neuron types in supragranular cortex in human and pronounced gradients as a function of cortical depth

findings.claim: Using single-cell multiomics data for the cerebellum from human, macaque, marmoset, and mouse, we revealed that TE contributions to different cell types are shaped by differences in evolutionary constraint across cell types, as well as the preferential co-option of certain TEs in specific cell states

findings.study_system: human, mouse, macaque, marmoset, rat, primate

conflicts.description: Whether rosehip cells are truly human-specific or have rodent homologs. Boldog et al. 2018 identified rosehip cells as a novel human interneuron type with no clear mouse homolog, but subsequent transcriptomic studies suggest possible Lamp5/PAX6+ correspondences in mouse cortex.

The degree to which interneuron cell types are conserved vs divergent across mammalian species. While cardinal classes (PV, SST, VIP) are broadly conserved, the extent of within-class divergence in gene expression, proportions, and connectivity is debated. Hodge et al. 2019 and Bakken et al. 2021 highlight both conservation at the broad class level and significant species-specific divergence in marker expression and cell type proportions.

Whether double bouquet cells in primates have rodent homologs. Classical morphological studies (DeFelipe) argued these are primate-specific, while transcriptomic studies suggest they may correspond to specific VIP+ or calretinin+ subtypes in rodents.

The gap between mouse functional data and human disease relevance. Most functional characterization of interneuron types comes from mouse optogenetics, but the functional consequences of species-specific differences in proportions, laminar distribution, and gene expression remain largely untested in primates.

Disagreements on the proportions of different interneuron types across species. Human cortex appears to have relatively more CGE-derived (VIP, LAMP5) interneurons compared to mouse, but exact proportions vary considerably across studies depending on quantification method (immunohistochemistry vs. transcriptomics), cortical area, and laminar sampling.

figure_data.homogeneity_check: Definition of 'primate-specific' varies: some studies use strict absence in rodent transcriptomes, others use morphological criteria. Sequencing depth differences may lead to false absence calls.

evidence_gaps.description: Functional characterization of primate-specific interneuron subtypes (rosehip, double bouquet) is almost entirely absent. Most knowledge is morphological and transcriptomic with no in vivo functional data.
Systematic quantitative comparisons of interneuron proportions across multiple cortical areas within the same species are rare. Most studies sample one or a few areas.
Primate optogenetic studies targeting specific interneuron types are extremely limited. Nearly all circuit-level functional data comes from mouse.
The functional consequences of different PV:SST:VIP ratios across species have not been empirically tested. Computational predictions exist but experimental validation is lacking.
Cross-species comparisons of interneuron connectivity patterns (beyond gene expression) are virtually absent. Whether conserved transcriptomic types make conserved circuit connections is unknown.

unreplicated_claims.claim: Rosehip cells are a human cortex-specific GABAergic cell type with no mouse homolog
unreplicated_claims.note: The initial Boldog et al. 2018 characterization used human tissue only. While subsequent transcriptomic atlases have mapped similar cells, independent morpho-electric characterization from another lab using the same criteria has not been published.

unreplicated_claims.claim: Double bouquet cells are strictly primate-specific with no rodent equivalent
unreplicated_claims.note: Classical claim from DeFelipe and colleagues. Recent transcriptomic work suggests possible rodent correspondences but morphological equivalence remains undemonstrated.

unreplicated_claims.claim: Human cortex has proportionally more CGE-derived interneurons than mouse cortex
unreplicated_claims.note: Hodge et al. 2019 and subsequent BICCN studies support this, but exact proportions vary significantly by method and area.
